title: abelian-difference-set-425-160-60-group-5-85
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only formalize a nonterminal quotient-profile theorem. The publication packet is not seal-ready until the induced 16-layer design problem in C_5^2 is resolved into an existence or nonexistence theorem.
micro_paper_assessment: The verified 0/10 C_17 layer-profile theorem is a real exact slice, not just an example, but it is still too internal to carry the micro-paper lane by itself. Without a direct closure of the induced 16-layer obstruction, this remains a slice rather than a human-ready note.
family_affinity: High affinity with abelian residual-row problems in direct products C_5^2 x C_17.
generalization_signal: The quotient-centering reduction should transfer to related direct-product rows, though closure is not yet general.
strongest_honest_claim: Up to translation in C_17, any (425,160,60)-difference set in C_5^2 x C_17 must avoid exactly one C_5^2-coset and meet each of the other sixteen C_5^2-cosets in exactly 10 points.
paper_title_hint: A Forced 0/10 C_17-Layer Profile for (425,160,60) Difference Sets in C_5^2 x C_17
candidate_theorem_slice: Any putative (425,160,60)-difference set in C_5^2 x C_17 has, up to translation in C_17, the forced layer profile (0,10,10,...,10), reducing the original existence question to an induced 16-layer design obstruction inside C_5^2.
next_action: Take one direct, no-fallback pass at the induced family of sixteen 10-subsets A_q in C_5^2. If that does not close the existence question quickly, move this candidate aside rather than expanding it into a broader program.
